dateline: Seoul, South Korea — Police Seek Arrest of HYBE Chairman Bang Si-hyuk; BTS North America Tour Opens April 25
heroHeadline: HYBE Chairman Bang Si-hyuk Faces Arrest Warrant as BTS Prepares for Tampa Tour Opener
heroSubtitle: 4,695 days since debut: Seoul police announced on April 21 they are seeking an arrest warrant for HYBE Chairman Bang Si-hyuk over alleged ₩190 billion ($129M) IPO fraud — the most significant legal crisis in HYBE's history. BTS opens the North American leg of the ARIRANG World Tour at Raymond James Stadium, Tampa, FL on April 25–28 (4 sold-out nights, ~220,000 fans). ARIRANG holds No. 3 on the Billboard 200 in week 4 (Apr 25 chart, 78K units) — best fourth-week chart position in K-pop history.
footerNote: Data sourced from HYBE, Billboard, Hanteo Chart, Gaon Chart, Melon, Spotify, Netflix, Guinness World Records, IFPI, Korea Times, Yonhap, setlist.fm, Japan Times, Starnews Korea, Korea Herald, Bloomberg, Variety, UPI, and Recording Academy.
